2606.12703.title: SMSR: Certified Defence Against Runtime Memory Poisoning in Persistent LLM Agent Systems
2606.12703.paper_summary: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) agents increasingly run with persistent memory that accumulates across user sessions. This creates a new attack surface: an adversary interacting only through normal channels can inject crafted memories that, once retrieved, steer the agent's responses for future users, without touching model weights or code. We call this Multi-Session Memory Poisoning (MSMP) and show that no existing defence certifies against it; static-corpus defences (RobustRAG, ReliabilityRAG) assume a fixed knowledge base, and heuristic filters are bypassed by fluent enterprise-style text. We present Signed Memory with Smoothed Retrieval (SMSR), the first defence with a certified robustness bound for this setting. Component 1 adds HMAC-SHA256 provenance at write time, blocking unsigned injection. Component 2 applies randomised memory ablation with verdict-based majority voting at query time, bounding the influence of authenticated adversaries. We prove that no provenance-free retrieval-time filter can certify against adaptive injection, derive a hypergeometric certificate for Component 2, and formalise the Consistent Minority Effect, whereby a consistent adversarial answer wins string-based voting as a numerical minority while verdict-based voting removes it. Across 15 enterprise scenarios (3,150 repeated trials), Component 1 cuts attack success from 93-100% to 0% for all unsigned variants. For an authenticated adversary with a single injection, Component 2 holds success to 8.0% (95% CI [5.8, 10.9], n=450), below the certified worst case. In an end-to-end query-only attack where the agent itself writes the poison rather than it being pre-seeded, SMSR reduces success from 65.3% to 5.3% (n=150, non-overlapping CIs) on a live agent stack. Clean-query utility is 90% (Component 1) and 85% (combined).
2606.12703.paper_summary_zh: 檢索增強生成（RAG）代理越來越多地使用持久記憶，這些記憶會在用戶會話中累積。這創造了一個新的攻擊面：一個僅通過正常渠道互動的對手可以注入精心製作的記憶，一旦被檢索，便會引導代理對未來用戶的回應，而無需觸及模型權重或代碼。我們稱之為多會話記憶毒化（MSMP），並顯示現有的防禦措施無法對其進行認證；靜態語料庫防禦（RobustRAG、ReliabilityRAG）假設一個固定的知識基礎，而流利的企業風格文本則繞過了啟發式過濾器。我們提出了具有平滑檢索的簽名記憶（SMSR），這是針對這一設置的第一個具有認證穩健性界限的防禦。組件1在寫入時添加HMAC-SHA256來源，阻止未簽名的注入。組件2在查詢時應用隨機記憶消融，並基於判決的多數投票，限制經過身份驗證的對手的影響。我們證明，沒有來源的檢索時間過濾器可以對自適應注入進行認證，為組件2推導出超幾何證書，並形式化一致性少數效應，即一致的對抗性答案在基於字符串的投票中獲勝，作為數值少數，而基於判決的投票則將其移除。在15個企業場景（3,150次重複試驗）中，組件1將攻擊成功率從93-100%降低到0%，適用於所有未簽名變體。對於具有單次注入的經過身份驗證的對手，組件2將成功率控制在8.0%（95% CI [5.8, 10.9], n=450），低於認證的最壞情況。在一個端到端的僅查詢攻擊中，代理本身寫入毒素而不是預先注入，SMSR將成功率從65.3%降低到5.3%（n=150，非重疊的CI）。清潔查詢的效用為90%（組件1）和85%（組合）。
2606.12703.author: Tarun Sharma et.al.
